claimText: Supraglacial lakes form in depressions on glaciers, whereas moraine-dammed lakes form at glacier toes and are highly prone to sudden failure.
evidenceExcerpt: Supraglacial lakes form in depressions on glaciers, while moraine-dammed lakes form at glacier toes and are highly prone to sudden failure.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing the formation and risk profiles of different types of glacial lakes.
distractorHooks: Swapping the definitions by stating supraglacial lakes form at glacier toes
Claiming supraglacial lakes are the ones most prone to sudden failure instead of moraine-dammed lakes

claimText: Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) can be triggered by glacial surging, moraine dam failure, seismic activities, and human activities.
evidenceExcerpt: GLOFs can be triggered by glacial surging (rapid short-term advance of a glacier), moraine dam failure, seismic activities, and human activities.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying the natural and anthropogenic triggers of GLOFs.
distractorHooks: Stating that human activities cannot trigger GLOFs
Confusing glacial surging (advance) with glacial retreat as the direct trigger mechanism

claimText: The Central Water Commission (CWC) monitors glacial lakes and water bodies in the Himalayas.
evidenceExcerpt: The Central Water Commission (CWC) monitors 902 glacial lakes and water bodies in the Himalayas.
examUse: Institution-feature question identifying the nodal agency responsible for monitoring Himalayan glacial lakes.
distractorHooks: Attributing the monitoring responsibility to the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)
Attributing the monitoring to the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) or Geological Survey of India (GSI)

claimText: The National GLOF Risk Mitigation Project (NGRMP) covers the states of Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Sikkim, and Uttarakhand.
evidenceExcerpt: The National GLOF Risk Mitigation Project (NGRMP) covers the states of Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Sikkim, and Uttarakhand.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the geographical coverage of the NGRMP.
distractorHooks: Including Jammu & Kashmir or Ladakh in the project coverage
Including non-Himalayan North-Eastern states like Assam or Meghalaya

claimText: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery is utilized to automatically detect changes in the size of glacial lakes.
evidenceExcerpt: Synthetic Aperture Radar imagery is utilized to automatically detect changes in the size of glacial lakes.
examUse: Statement-based question on the application of SAR technology in disaster management and glacial monitoring.
distractorHooks: Claiming that only optical satellite imagery can be used to detect changes in glacial lakes
Stating that SAR imagery is ineffective for glacial monitoring due to cloud cover
